summary: Gruber writes a brief personal note to readers explaining Daring Fireball's unusual week-long silence. He acknowledges the timing is particularly notable given it's new iPhone launch week. Without disclosing specifics, he assures readers he's dealing with a personal situation but is OK and recovering. He expresses eagerness to return to writing and promises iPhone reviews soon, closing with gratitude for readers' patience.
key_points: Daring Fireball went silent for a full week, which Gruber acknowledges is highly unusual for the site
A personal situation kept him offline, though he assures readers he will be OK
The timing coincides with new iPhone launch week, making the absence especially conspicuous
The one-person nature of the site means any personal disruption halts all publishing
money_quotes: I have been dealing with — and working through — a very personal situation for the past week. It's OK. I'm going to be OK.
Given the one-man nature of this site, that has meant that nothing has been published.
I can feel it: I will be back soon. I'm itching to go.
I mean, jiminy, it's new iPhones week.
I so profoundly appreciate your patience and understanding.
key_insight: A rare moment of personal vulnerability from Gruber that inadvertently highlights both the fragility and authenticity of a one-person publishing operation.
